title: Italian NGE The Movie DTS Collectors Edition
display_title: Italian NGE The Movie DTS Collectors Edition
wikitext:
[]: Italian NGE The Movie DTS Collectors Edition             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Dec 27, 2010 10:30 pm Recently made a fascinating discovery while compiling an evangelion media release image index. Apparently the first 3000 DTS Collectors Edition copies came with a film strip of either Death and Rebirth or End of Evangelion. Also pictured in the article was an example of one of these strips. Clearly from Death. The website in question is here. http://www.animeotakuland.com/database/collettibili-anime/9147/neon-genesis-evangelion-the-features-film-dts-collector-s-edition/ What I want to know is if this applied to the Japanese release, or the Italian release only. Svenge, you bought the DTS collection. Does it say anything about a film strip anywhere? I'm not finding anything else online but this one image and claim. It would be VERY INTERESTING to find a strip of End of Evangelion as it is the last piece in the existential puzzle of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Dec 27, 2010 11:37 pm As far as I know, there was never any film strip on the R2J DTS movie collection. Mine doesn't have one, none of the ones I've seen on Yahoo.jp auctions has had one, and none of the retail sites ever made mention of it. The Italian release you linked to seems to be a pretty nice approximation of the original set, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Dec 28, 2010 12:11 am I see. So it didn't come in the original Japanese release. Odd that only Italians got a FILM STRIP of the movie, but oh well. I need to hunt down some Italians. That thread in the Rebuild section should be a good starting place. Next would be Italian auction sites... EDIT: Scattered PMs to the 4 winds and am on to Italian fan and auction sites

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Dec 28, 2010 10:12 am Clover wrote: Recently made a fascinating discovery while compiling an evangelion media release image index.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Dec 28, 2010 10:59 am -

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Dec 28, 2010 12:16 pm ath wrote: Great! Are you planning to contribute it to the Wiki? We talked about creating a page for the international releases in the past, but at the moment we only have one for the US releases .

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:31 pm @Username & svenge : Thanks! There's already a Summary of english video/DVD/blu-ray releases thread in the Editorium forum which has a lot of info, but I haven't compared its content with the new table yet. There's also the older and more general DVD/VHS Release Information thread, so I suggest to move this discussion there. We can create a new "Japanese Video Releases" page in the wiki by copying the layout of the "North American" page, or we can start from scratch with a table-based layout using your list. Which one do you prefer?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Dec 28, 2010 6:32 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Dec 28, 2010 6:54 pm Do as you see fit. I'm just interested in accurate information being widely available as a public service.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Wed Dec 29, 2010 9:05 am From svenge's table: Theatrical Edition (KIBA-105/6) 9/22/1999 w/ Dolby 5.1 audio [Disc 1: Death(True)² + Rebirth; Disc 2: End of Evangelion (Theatrical Version)]

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 29, 2010 9:06 am Just looked at the back cover of it, I think I may have made a mistake on that one, it's stereo only. :( I meant to put that note regarding Dolby 5.1 audio onto the KIBA-1009/10 listing (which I just re-confirmed on its back cover.) Fixed file and reuploaded here: https://docs.google.com/uc?id=0BzqOfVcPl2SqNTUzMzRlODktNGRkMS00Y2U3LWIxMjgtM2JjZDdiOWVlMTQ5&export=download&hl=en Hopefully I didn't make any more egregious errors :)

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Wed Dec 29, 2010 5:18 pm Does it look good enough? http://wiki.evageeks.org/Japanese_Video_Releases (I know, the code is a total mess and I definitely need to clean up by creating a new template, but I need to ask someone who knows MediaWiki better than me first)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 29, 2010 5:40 pm ath wrote: Does it look good enough? http://wiki.evageeks.org/Japanese_Video_Releases

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 29, 2010 6:07 pm Clover wrote: Needs Genesis and rental copies (KIBA-1000X series)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 29, 2010 6:22 pm svenge wrote: Here's the rental DVD data (note that I'm not sure about the release dates on these) Note that they split up the movies into 2 separate releases for the rental market. This contrasts with the KIBA-105/6 and KIBA-1009/10 retail releases, which had both movies together.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 29, 2010 6:50 pm Clover wrote: Do you have scans of KIBA-10001, KIBA-10004, KIBA-10005, KIBA-10006, and KIBR-9/10? Do you know if the covers of KIBA-1001-8 were changed for the KIBR release? I wouldn't need scans if the only difference was a little serial number and a "rental only" label.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Sat Jan 01, 2011 5:24 pm svenge wrote: Edit: I just re-updated the master file w/ the Japanese rental DVDs and laserdisc data. https://docs.google.com/uc?id=0BzqOfVcPl2SqNTUzMzRlODktNGRkMS00Y2U3LWIxMjgtM2JjZDdiOWVlMTQ5&export=download&hl=en

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Jan 01, 2011 5:27 pm I think that LDDB has that kind of technical data for the LDs. http://www.lddb.com/search.php?search=evangelion&sort=title&format=ld

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Fri Jan 21, 2011 1:57 pm Clover wrote: I see. So it didn't come in the original Japanese release. Odd that only Italians got a FILM STRIP of the movie, but oh well. I need to hunt down some Italians. That thread in the Rebuild section should be a good starting place. Next would be Italian auction sites... EDIT: Scattered PMs to the 4 winds and am on to Italian fan and auction sites

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Jan 22, 2011 12:43 am gwern wrote: It's odd, but Italy seems to be very well-favored from an anime perspective.
